Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-15 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Output Today's data reveals two exceptionally strong short-term momentum performers leading the 700+ ticker universe. NBR ranks #1 with a composite score of 96.9, driven by extraordinary returns across all timeframes—224.51% over one year, 117.32% over six months, and 54.58% over three months—while maintaining pristine technical structure (all four major moving averages above price). PUMP follows with similarly dominant momentum characteristics, posting 188.43% one-year returns and 67.31% six-month returns, though its score appears slightly lower based on ratio consistency. Both stocks pass ratio quality tests and demonstrate resilience during SPY downdays, with NBR showing particular strength (bad_spy_score of 2.87). The standout observation is NBR's extreme correlation with energy sector (XLE at 0.542), suggesting today's rally is concentrated in commodity-linked, trend-following names that have maintained clean technical setups. Neither asset shows pullback signals, indicating the momentum structure remains intact as of this snapshot.
